session.h(3) Library Functions Manual session.h(3)

NAME

session.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct ring_consensus_t
Ring consensus state (Host-Mediated Proactive Election). struct host_liveness_t
Host liveness detection state. struct migration_ctx_t
Host migration context for tracking failover state. struct discovery_session_t
Discovery session context. struct discovery_config_t
Configuration for discovery session.

Macros


#define MAX_PARTICIPANTS 16

Typedefs


typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)
Callback to check if session should exit.

Enumerations


enum migration_state_t { MIGRATION_STATE_NONE, MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE }
Migration detection and failover state. enum discovery_state_t { DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS, DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED }
Discovery session state.

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Definition at line 35 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)

Callback to check if session should exit. Called periodically during blocking operations (e.g., ACDS connect, join). Should return true to gracefully cancel the current operation.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

true to cancel operation, false to continue

Definition at line 113 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum discovery_state_t

Discovery session state.

Enumerator

DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT
Initial state.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS
Connecting to ACDS.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION
Creating new session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION
Joining existing session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER
Waiting for peer to join (initiator).
DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING
NAT negotiation in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST
Starting as host.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST
Connecting to host as participant.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE
Session active (call in progress).
DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING
Host migration in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED
Session failed.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED
Session ended.

enum migration_state_t


Migration detection and failover state. SIMPLIFIED DESIGN:
    No re-election during migration! Future host was pre-elected 5 minutes ago.
    When current host dies, we already know who takes over and where to find
    them.


Enumerator
  





  
MIGRATION_STATE_NONE
    

  
No migration in progress.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED
    

  
Host disconnect detected.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER
    

  
Failing over to pre-elected future host.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE
    

  
Failover complete, call resumed.

89   MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, 
90   MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, 
91   MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE  
92 } migration_state_t;

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Become the host (called when elected as future host).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 2345 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2345                                                                               {
2346   if (!session) {
2347     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2348     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2349   }
2350 
2351   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2352            session->participant_id[1]);
2353 
2354   // Get configured port (or use default)
2355   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2356 
2357   // Mark ourselves as host
2358   session->is_host = true;
2359 
2360   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2361   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2362     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2363         .port = host_port,
2364         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2365         .max_clients = 32,
2366         .encryption_enabled = true,
2367     };
2368 
2369     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2370     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2371       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2372       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2373     }
2374 
2375     // Start listening for new participant connections
2376     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2377     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2378       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2379       return hstart;
2380     }
2381 
2382     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2383   }
2384 
2385   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2386   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2387   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2388   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2389   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2390   // TODO: Use actual determined host address instead of 127.0.0.1
2391   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2392   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2393   // TODO: Use actual connection type based on NAT quality
2394 
2395   if (session->acds_transport) {
2396     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
2397                               sizeof(announcement), 0);
2398     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2399              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2400   }
2401 
2402   // Mark migration complete
2403   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2404 
2405   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2406   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2407 
2408   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2409 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2222 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2222                                                                                    {
2223   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2224     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2225     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2226   }
2227 
2228   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2229   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2230     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2231     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2232       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2233     }
2234     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2235     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2236   }
2237 
2238   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2239   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2240   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2241     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2242   }
2243 
2244   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2245   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2246     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2247   }
2248 
2249   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2250 
2251   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2252   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2253     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2254     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2255       // Ping timed out
2256       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2257       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2258       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2259                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2260 
2261       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2262       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2263         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2264         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2265       }
2266     }
2267   }
2268 
2269   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2270   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2271   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2272     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2273     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2274     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2275       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2276       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2277         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2278         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2279         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2280                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2281       } else {
2282         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2283         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2284         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2285           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2286           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2287         }
2288       }
2289     }
2290   }
2291 
2292   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2293 }

2411                                                                                          {
2412   if (!session) {
2413     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2414     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2415   }
2416 
2417   log_info("Reconnecting to future host: %s:%u (connection type: %u)", session->ring.future_host_address,
2418            session->ring.future_host_port, session->ring.future_host_connection_type);
2419 
2420   // Destroy old participant context if present
2421   if (session->participant_ctx) {
2422     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2423     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
2424   }
2425 
2426   // Create new participant context for the future host
2427   session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
2428       .address = session->ring.future_host_address,
2429       .port = session->ring.future_host_port,
2430       .enable_audio = true,
2431       .enable_video = true,
2432       .encryption_enabled = true,
2433   };
2434 
2435   session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
2436   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2437     log_error("Failed to create participant context for future host");
2438     return ERROR_MEMORY;
2439   }
2440

2442   asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);

2444     log_error("Failed to connect to future host: %d", pconn);
2445     return pconn;
2446   }
2447 
2448   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2449 
2450   // Update our host info with the new future host details
2451   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);
2452   SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->ring.future_host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
2453   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;
2454 
2455   log_info("Updated host info to: %s:%u (id: %02x%02x...)", session->host_address, session->host_port,
2456            session->host_id[0], session->host_id[1]);
2457 
2458   // Mark migration complete (in real implementation, this would be done after successful connection)
2459   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2460 
2461   // Transition to ACTIVE state (participant is ready)
2462   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2463 
2464   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2465 }

Parameters


Config Module Session
  configuration


Returns


New session or NULL on error

52                                                                                 {
53   if (!config) {
54     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
55     return NULL;
56   }
57 
58   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);
59   if (!session) {
60     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate discovery session");
61     return NULL;
62   }
63 
64   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;
65   session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
66   session->acds_transport = NULL;
67   session->peer_manager = NULL;
68   session->webrtc_transport_ready = false;
69   session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
70   session->webrtc_retry_attempt = 0;
71   session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = 0;
72   session->stun_servers = NULL;
73   session->stun_count = 0;
74   session->turn_servers = NULL;
75   session->turn_count = 0;
76 
77   // Initialize host liveness detection
78   session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = 0;
79   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
80   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
81   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
82   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
83   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
84   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
85 
86   log_info("discovery_session_create: After CALLOC - participant_ctx=%p, host_ctx=%p", session->participant_ctx,
87            session->host_ctx);
88 
89   // Load or generate identity key
90   char identity_path[256];
91   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
92   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
93     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
94     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
95       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
96       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
97       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
98       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
99         // Save for future use
100         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
101       }
102     }
103   }
104 
105   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
106     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
107     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
108     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
109   } else {
110     char fingerprint[65];
111     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
112     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
113   }
114 
115   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
116   const options_t *opts = options_get();
117   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
118     // Load all identity keys from options
119     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
120       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
121       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
122       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
123       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
124         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
125         char fingerprint[65];
126         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
127         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
128       } else {
129         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
130       }
131     }
132     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
133     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
134   } else {
135     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
136     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
137     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
138   }
139 
140   // ACDS connection info
141   if (config->acds_url && config->acds_url[0]) {
142     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_url, config->acds_url, sizeof(session->acds_url));
143   }
144   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
145     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
146   } else {
147     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
148   }
149   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
150 
151   // Session string (if joining)
152   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
153     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
154     session->is_initiator = false;
155   } else {
156     session->is_initiator = true;
157   }
158 
159   // Callbacks
160   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
161   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
162   session->on_error = config->on_error;
163   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
164   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
165   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
166 
167   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
168             session->acds_port);
169 
170   return session;
171 }

175     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");

177   }
178 
179   // Close ACDS connection
180   if (session->acds_transport) {
181     acip_transport_destroy(session->acds_transport);
182     session->acds_transport = NULL;
183   }
184   if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
185     socket_close(session->acds_socket);
186     session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
187   }
188 
189   // Destroy host context if active
190   if (session->host_ctx) {
191     session_host_destroy(session->host_ctx);
192     session->host_ctx = NULL;
193   }
194 
195   // Destroy participant context if active
196   if (session->participant_ctx) {
197     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
198     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
199   }
200 
201   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (NEW)
202   if (session->peer_manager) {
203     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
204     session->peer_manager = NULL;
205   }
206 
207   // Clean up STUN/TURN server arrays (NEW)
208   if (session->stun_servers) {
209     SAFE_FREE(session->stun_servers);
210     session->stun_servers = NULL;
211     session->stun_count = 0;
212   }
213 
214   if (session->turn_servers) {
215     SAFE_FREE(session->turn_servers);
216     session->turn_servers = NULL;
217     session->turn_count = 0;
218   }
219 
220   SAFE_FREE(session);
221 }

  (discovery_session_t * session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)


Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host.


Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover


Definition at line 2295 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2295                                                                                                                      {
2296   if (!session) {
2297     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2298     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2299   }
2300 
2301   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2302 
2303   // Initialize migration context
2304   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2305   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2306   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2307   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2308 
2309   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2310   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2311   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2312   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2313   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2314   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2315   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2316 
2317   packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost), 0);
2318 
2319   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2320   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2321     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2322     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2323     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2324     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2325     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2326   }
2327 
2328   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2329   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2330   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2331 
2332   // Check if I am the future host
2333   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2334     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2335     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2336     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2337   } else {
2338     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2339     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2340              session->ring.future_host_port);
2341     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2342   }
2343 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1514 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1514                                                                                               {
1515   if (!session) {
1516     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1517   }
1518 
1519   // Handle state-specific processing
1520   switch (session->state) {
1521   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1522     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1523     if (session->acds_transport && timeout_ns > 0) {
1524       // Set socket timeout
1525       if (socket_set_timeout(session->acds_socket, timeout_ns) == 0) {
1526         packet_type_t type;
1527         void *data = NULL;
1528         size_t len = 0;
1529         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1530 
1531         // Try to receive a packet (non-blocking with timeout)
1532         asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1533             packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1534         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1535           if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1536             log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1537             set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1538             buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1539           } else {
1540             // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1541             log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1542             buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1543           }
1544         } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1545           // Timeout is normal, just return
1546         } else {
1547           log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1548         }
1549       }
1550     } else {
1551       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid transport
1552       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1553     }
1554     break;
1555   }
1556 
1557   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1558     // Handle NAT negotiation
1559     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1560       // Initialize negotiation
1561       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1562       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1563 
1564       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS immediately (let peer receive it)
1565       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1566       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1567         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1568         // Don't fail - we'll try again next iteration
1569       }
1570     }
1571 
1572     // Try to receive peer's NETWORK_QUALITY from ACDS (non-blocking with short timeout)
1573     if (!session->negotiate.peer_quality_received) {
1574       asciichat_error_t recv_result = receive_network_quality_from_acds(session);
1575       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1576         // Got peer quality - proceed to election
1577         log_info("Received peer NETWORK_QUALITY, proceeding to host election");
1578       } else if (recv_result != ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1579         // Real error (not just timeout)
1580         log_debug("Error receiving NETWORK_QUALITY: %d (this might be normal if peer not ready)", recv_result);
1581       }
1582     }
1583 
1584     // Check if we have both qualities and can determine host
1585     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1586       // Both sides have exchanged NAT quality - determine the winner
1587       log_info("Both NAT qualities received, determining host...");
1588       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1589       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1590         // Store result
1591         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1592 
1593         if (session->is_host) {
1594           // We won the negotiation - become the host
1595           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1596                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1597                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1598           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1599           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1600           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1601           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1602         } else {
1603           // They won - we'll be a participant
1604           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1605                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1606                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1607                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1608           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1609           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1610           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1611         }
1612       } else {
1613         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1614         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1615       }
1616     } else {
1617       // Still waiting for peer quality - sleep briefly to avoid busy loop
1618       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1619     }
1620     break;
1621   }
1622 
1623   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1624     // Start hosting
1625     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1626       // Create host context with configured port
1627       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1628 
1629       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1630           .port = host_port,
1631           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1632           .max_clients = 32,
1633           .encryption_enabled = true,
1634       };
1635 
1636       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1637       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1638         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1639         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1640         break;
1641       }
1642 
1643       // Start listening
1644       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1645       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1646         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1647         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1648         break;
1649       }
1650 
1651       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1652     }
1653 
1654     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1655     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1656       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1657     }
1658 
1659     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1660     break;
1661   }
1662 
1663   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1664     // Connect to host as participant
1665 
1666     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1667     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1668       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1669       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1670         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1671         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1672         break;
1673       }
1674 
1675       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1676       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1677         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1678                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1679 
1680         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1681         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1682             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1683 
1684         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1685           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1686           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1687           break;
1688         }
1689 
1690         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1691         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1692         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() / 1000;
1693       }
1694 
1695       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1696       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1697         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1698         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1699 
1700         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1701           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1702                     timeout_ms);
1703 
1704           // Check if we have retries remaining
1705           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1706           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1707             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1708             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1709 
1710             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1711                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1712 
1713             // Wait for backoff period
1714             platform_sleep_ms(backoff_ms);
1715 
1716             // Destroy old peer manager
1717             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1718             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1719 
1720             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1721             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1722             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1723               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1724               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1725 
1726               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1727               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1728                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1729                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1730                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1731               }
1732 
1733               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1734             }
1735 
1736             // Reset connection state for retry
1737             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1738             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1739 
1740             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1741                      max_attempts + 1);
1742           } else {
1743             // No more retries - connection failed
1744             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1745 
1746             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1747             char error_msg[256];
1748             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1749                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1750             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1751 
1752             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1753             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1754               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1755               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1756               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1757             }
1758 
1759             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1760           }
1761         }
1762       }
1763 
1764       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1765       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1766       if (session->acds_transport) {
1767         // Check if data available (non-blocking with timeout_ns)
1768         struct timeval tv_timeout;
1769         tv_timeout.tv_sec = timeout_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
1770         tv_timeout.tv_usec = (timeout_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT) / 1000;
1771 
1772         fd_set readfds;
1773         FD_ZERO(&readfds);
1774         FD_SET(session->acds_socket, &readfds);
1775 
1776         int select_result = select(session->acds_socket + 1, &readfds, NULL, NULL, &tv_timeout);
1777         if (select_result > 0 && FD_ISSET(session->acds_socket, &readfds)) {
1778           // Data available to read
1779           packet_type_t type;
1780           void *data = NULL;
1781           size_t len = 0;
1782           void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1783 
1784           asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1785               packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1786 
1787           if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1788             // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1789             handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1790             buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1791           } else {
1792             log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1793           }
1794         }
1795       }
1796 
1797       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1798       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1799       break;
1800     }
1801 
1802     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1803       // Create participant context for this session
1804       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1805           .address = session->host_address,
1806           .port = session->host_port,
1807           .enable_audio = true,
1808           .enable_video = true,
1809           .encryption_enabled = true,
1810       };
1811 
1812       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1813       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1814         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1815         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1816         break;
1817       }
1818 
1819       // Attempt connection
1820       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1821       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1822         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1823         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1824         break;
1825       }
1826 
1827       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1828 
1829       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1830       // Get the socket from participant context
1831       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1832       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1833         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
1834         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
1835         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1836         break;
1837       }
1838 
1839       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
1840       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
1841       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1842       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
1843       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
1844       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
1845       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1846       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1847       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1848 
1849       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
1850       if (result != 0) {
1851         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
1852         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
1853         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1854         break;
1855       }
1856 
1857       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
1858       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
1859       packet_type_t packet_type;
1860       void *payload = NULL;
1861       size_t payload_len = 0;
1862 
1863       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
1864       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1865         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
1866         if (payload) {
1867           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1868         }
1869         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
1870         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1871         break;
1872       }
1873 
1874       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
1875         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
1876         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1877         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
1878         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1879         break;
1880       }
1881 
1882       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
1883       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
1884       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1885 
1886       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
1887         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
1888         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
1889         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1890         break;
1891       }
1892 
1893       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
1894                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
1895 
1896       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
1897       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
1898       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
1899       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1900       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
1901       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1902       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
1903       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1904       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
1905       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1906 
1907       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
1908       if (result != 0) {
1909         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1910         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1911         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1912         break;
1913       }
1914 
1915       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
1916       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
1917     }
1918 
1919     // Check if connection is established
1920     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
1921     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
1922     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
1923               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
1924 
1925     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
1926       // Log more details about why it's not connected
1927       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
1928     }
1929 
1930     if (is_connected) {
1931       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1932       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1933     } else {
1934       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
1935                 "is_connected=%d)",
1936                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
1937     }
1938     break;
1939   }
1940 
1941   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
1942     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
1943     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_transport) {
1944       // Use non-blocking polling to check for incoming ACDS packets
1945       struct pollfd pfd = {.fd = session->acds_socket, .events = POLLIN, .revents = 0};
1946 
1947       int poll_result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, 0); // Non-blocking poll (timeout=0)
1948 
1949       if (poll_result > 0 && (pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
1950         // Data available to read
1951         packet_type_t type;
1952         void *data = NULL;
1953         size_t len = 0;
1954         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1955 
1956         asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1957             packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1958 
1959         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1960           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1961           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1962           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1963         }
1964       }
1965     }
1966 
1967     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
1968     if (!session->is_host) {
1969       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
1970       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
1971       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1972         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
1973         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
1974 
1975         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
1976         const options_t *opts = options_get();
1977         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
1978           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1979           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1980           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
1981           return ERROR_NETWORK;
1982         }
1983 
1984         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
1985         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
1986         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
1987       }
1988     }
1989     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1990     break;
1991 
1992   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
1993     // Host migration in progress
1994     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
1995     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
1996       // Migration completed successfully
1997       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1998       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
1999     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
2000       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
2001       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
2002       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
2003       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
2004     }
2005     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
2006     break;
2007 
2008   default:
2009     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state");
2010     break;
2011   }
2012 
2013   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2014 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 1325 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1325                                                                         {
1326   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1327 
1328   if (!session) {
1329     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1330   }
1331 
1332   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1333            session->session_string);
1334 
1335   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1336   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1337     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1338     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1339   }
1340 
1341   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1342   // Connect to ACDS
1343   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1344   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1345     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1346     return result;
1347   }
1348   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1349 
1350   // Check again after connection
1351   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1352     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1353     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1354   }
1355 
1356   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1357   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1358   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1359   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1360 
1361   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1362   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1363     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1364     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1365     return result;
1366   }
1367 
1368   // Store identity key in handshake context
1369   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1370   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1371   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1372   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1373 
1374   // Step 0: Receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS from ACDS
1375   {
1376     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1377     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1378     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1379     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1380 
1381     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1382     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
1383       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS (got type %u)", pkt_type);
1384       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1385       set_error(session, result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
1386                 "Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS");
1387       return result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1388     }
1389 
1390     if (pkt_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
1391       log_error("discovery_session_start: Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size: %zu (expected %zu)", pkt_len,
1392                 sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1393       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1394       set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size");
1395       return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1396     }
1397 
1398     crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
1399     memcpy(&server_params, pkt_data, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1400     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1401 
1402     // Pass params in network byte order — crypto_handshake_set_parameters
1403     // handles conversion internally for client (is_server=false)
1404     result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&handshake_ctx, &server_params);
1405     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1406       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to set crypto parameters");
1407       set_error(session, result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
1408       return result;
1409     }
1410     log_info("discovery_session_start: CRYPTO_PARAMETERS received and applied");
1411   }
1412 
1413   // Step 1: Key exchange - receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT and respond
1414   {
1415     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1416     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1417     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1418     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1419 
1420     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1421     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1422       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
1423       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange recv failed");
1424       return result;
1425     }
1426 
1427     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1428     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1429 
1430     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1431       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1432       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1433       return result;
1434     }
1435   }
1436 
1437   // Step 2: Auth response - receive AUTH_CHALLENGE and respond
1438   {
1439     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1440     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1441     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1442     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1443 
1444     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1445     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1446       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive AUTH_CHALLENGE");
1447       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response recv failed");
1448       return result;
1449     }
1450 
1451     result =
1452         crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1453     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1454 
1455     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1456       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake auth response failed");
1457       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response failed");
1458       return result;
1459     }
1460   }
1461 
1462   // Step 3: Complete handshake
1463   // Note: Some servers (like ACDS without auth) complete the handshake in step 2,
1464   // so check if we're already done before trying to receive another packet
1465   if (handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1466     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1467     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1468     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1469     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1470 
1471     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1472     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1473       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE");
1474       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete recv failed");
1475       return result;
1476     }
1477 
1478     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1479     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1480 
1481     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1482       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake complete failed");
1483       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete failed");
1484       return result;
1485     }
1486   } else {
1487     log_info("discovery_session_start: Handshake already complete from auth_response step");
1488   }
1489 
1490   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1491 
1492   // Check again after handshake
1493   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1494     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1495     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1496   }
1497 
1498   // Create or join session
1499   if (session->is_initiator) {
1500     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1501     result = create_session(session);
1502     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1503              session->participant_ctx, result);
1504     return result;
1505   } else {
1506     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1507     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);
1508     log_info("discovery_session_start: join_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, state=%d, result=%d",
1509              session->participant_ctx, session->state, result);
1510     return result;
1511   }
1512 }

  discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t * session)

Parameters


Session Session context

2016                                                           {
2017   if (!session) {
2018     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2019     return;
2020   }
2021 
2022   log_info("Stopping discovery session...");
2023 
2024   // Send SESSION_LEAVE if connected
2025   if (session->acds_transport && session->session_id[0] != 0) {
2026     acip_session_leave_t leave_msg;
2027     memset(&leave_msg, 0, sizeof(leave_msg));
2028     memcpy(leave_msg.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2029     memcpy(leave_msg.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2030 
2031     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_LEAVE, &leave_msg, sizeof(leave_msg),
2032                               0);
2033   }
2034 
2035   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED);
